critic_raw:
## ML Critique: Clinical Transformer vs. TF-IDF for Multi-Label ICD-10 Coding

**Issue 1 — Stratified random split violates temporal validity for ICD-10 coding**

ICD-10 coding practices evolve over time: payer policy updates, CMS guidelines changes, and code definition revisions all affect coding patterns. A stratified random split over a 3-year dataset allows future coding patterns (year 3) into training while earlier patterns (year 1) appear in test. The model learns future coding conventions it would not have seen at the time of the training cutoff. A temporal split (train on year 1-2, validate on year 2, test on year 3) is required for valid prospective evaluation.

*Evidence:* Compare AUPRC under random vs. temporal split. Any decline for the transformer (which exploits coding evolution patterns in clinical text) indicates temporal leakage.

**Issue 2 — Test set used for per-label threshold selection**

ICD-10 coding involves hundreds of labels with heterogeneous prevalence. Selecting per-label decision thresholds on the test set after training incorporates test data into the model development pipeline. This is evaluation contamination: the thresholds are tuned to the test distribution, inflating precision and recall at the reported operating point. Threshold selection is a hyperparameter tuning step that must occur on the validation set only.

*Evidence:* Compare micro-averaged F1 under test-set-selected thresholds vs. validation-set-selected thresholds applied to the test set. Any improvement from test-set selection = contamination.
defender_raw:
## Design Defense: ICD-10 Coding

**Pass 1:**

Issue 1: ICD-10 coding practices evolve. A random split allows future coding conventions into training. Temporal validity requires chronological split. Acknowledged.

Issue 2: Per-label threshold selection on the test set is evaluation contamination. Selecting thresholds that optimize performance on the test distribution inflates reported metrics. Must be done on validation set only. Acknowledged.

**Pass 2:** Both issues are concrete. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
## Adjudication — isolated_debate, Run 1

Both issues acknowledged and conceded by defender.

**Verdict: critique_wins**
